transcript: maladaptive daydreaming a silent trap or a hidden gift
have you ever found yourself pacing back and forth in your room completely lost in your dreams for hours
maybe with music playing in the background inspired by a movie scene or wishing to change a real life event
these dreams tend to repeat themselves eventually turning into a cycle that makes us forget reality
but why do we do this are we crazy haha of course not this phenomenon is called maladaptive daydreaming
maladaptive daydreaming refers to excessive uncontrolled daydreaming that takes up more time than real life
unlike regular daydreaming when someone enters this world they feel completely immersed in it disconnected from reality
yet many people are unaware that they or their loved ones might experience this condition
normally daydreaming fuels creativity but when it spirals out of control it can make daily life difficult
let me give you an example imagine going to the movies with your friends maybe watching avengers endgame or oppenheimer
you see an incredibly impactful scene like the final battle in endgame or oppenheimers realization of his new ideas

the moment resonates with you so deeply that you cant wait to get home and spend hours lost in thoughts about it instead of enjoying time with your friends you rush back to your imaginary world indulging in fantasies that dont really benefit you
in the end these dreams only provide temporary happiness while time slips away responsibilities pile up and socializing becomes more difficult

a more relatable example drifting off in class this is something i still struggle with just the other day during my english lesson i completely zoned out when my teacher ms gamze asked a question about the topic we had covered i simply replied i dont know i was probably daydreaming while you explained it
lets be honest long school hours make you want to escape and our dream worlds offer an irresistible refuge
now how do we differentiate between normal daydreaming and maladaptive daydreaming and how does this condition develop
i personally believe maladaptive daydreaming is closely linked to loneliness the covid 19 quarantine kept many people away from social life forcing them to retreat into their inner worlds
no one here can deny spending too much time on technology during lockdowns prolonged isolation affected our mental health triggering maladaptive daydreaming in many individuals
other factors include excessive exposure to technology from childhood traumatic experiences or simply having an overactive imagination

common signs of maladaptive daydreaming 1 the urge to walk while daydreaming expressing emotions physically if you find yourself pacing around your room acting out emotions intensely or even crying while lost in thought this is one of the biggest indicators

2 a need for isolation people with maladaptive daydreaming usually need to be alone to fully immerse themselves in their fantasies personally i cant even have my cat around when i daydream the presence of others disrupts the complete detachment from reality that maladaptive daydreamers seek

3 frequent loss of focus naturally someone constantly slipping into their imaginary world struggles with concentration in real life as you can see excessive daydreaming can negatively impact many areas of life
i first realized i had this issue in 7th grade and it hit me hard all my life i had these habits that i couldnt explain until i discovered they were symptoms of a condition
at first i didnt want to let go of it i mean think about it would you willingly leave a world where everything goes exactly how you want it just to face the harsh realities of real life of course not
but i confronted those realities and now i stand on this stage with confidence
how do you take control while it might be difficult to completely eliminate maladaptive daydreaming managing it is possible the key is to use your imagination to your advantage
instead of escaping into it turn it into motivation self discovery exploring new interests and socializing play a crucial role in this process

my suggestion engage in activities that involve communication speaking clubs debates and social responsibility projects help break the cycle of isolation and strengthen your connection with the real world

if you find yourself getting lost in daydreams too often take a step toward socializing i know stepping out of your comfort zone sounds scary but remember what i said about using your imagination to your advantage this is exactly where it comes in
what if i embarrass myself what if i fail so what the possibilities never end but unless you take the first step youll never get to experience them
otherwise youll remain stuck in your room distant from your family disconnected from your friends and struggling in your studies
who knows maybe by stepping outside your comfort zone youll discover your true passion and become a successful entrepreneur a diplomat or even next years speaker on this stage
taking such bold steps might be challenging for most people but not for us because in the worst case scenario we always have a place to retreat our dream world
thats exactly how you should use it to your advantage on your journey to self discovery learn how to harness your imagination
remember while excessive daydreaming can negatively affect your life once you learn to control it your dreams can become a powerful force that drives you
thanks
for listening
